item.title: U.S. launches "self-defense strikes" on Iran, says warships came under fire
item.description: As President Trump again voiced optimism that Iran will "make a deal" to end the war, Tehran declared itself the regulator of Strait of Hormuz shipping.

item.title: CBS News investigation reveals security gaps at hotel hosting White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: CBS News homeland security correspondent Nicole Sganga returned to the Hilton Hotel in Washington, D.C., to better understand the security surrounding the White House Correspondents' Dinner. What she found was a 13-minute gap in security camera coverage in the moments leading up to the shooting.

item.title: U.S. destroyers face second round of Iranian attacks
item.description: Two U.S. Navy destroyers transiting the Strait of Hormuz came under renewed attack Thursday, enduring what American officials described as a fiercer and more sustained Iranian assault than the barrage the warships faced only days earlier.

item.title: Tennessee approves congressional map carving up majority-Black district
item.description: Tennessee lawmakers approved a new congressional map on Thursday that carves up a majority-Black district in Memphis. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe reports.

item.title: Justice Department says possible Epstein suicide note hasn't yet been authenticated
item.description: A federal judge has made public an apparent suicide note written by convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. CBS News' Anna Schecter reports.

item.title: Can AI help you figure out how much money you need to retire?
item.description: As more people turn to chatbots for financial advice, experts say AI offers both pros and cons for retirement planning. Here's what to know.

item.title: Tennessee approves new congressional map dissolving majority Black district
item.description: Tennessee Republicans earlier Thursday approved a measure to overturn the state's ban on mid-decade redistricting.

item.title: iPhone owners could get up to $95 from a new Apple settlement
item.description: A proposed $250 million settlement would compensate millions of consumers who bought the iPhone 16, iPhone 15 Pro or iPhone 15 Pro Max.

item.title: Trump administration waiting for Iranian response to peace proposal
item.description: The war with Iran has become a waiting game, with Tehran saying it's reviewing the most recent U.S. proposal, and the White House tamping down aggression in an effort to reach a deal. CBS News' Nancy Cordes and Samantha Vinograd have the latest.

item.title: Google unveils screenless Fitbit Air to compete with Whoop
item.description: Fitness trackers started as devices for measuring workouts, but now they are designed for 24/7 monitoring and the passive collection of health data. Fitbit announced its latest device, called the Fitbit Air, to compete with other screenless trackers like the Whoop. Tech journalist Lexi Savvides joins CBS News with more.

item.title: State Department reviewing all Mexican consulates in U.S. as tensions grow
item.description: The State Department is initiating a review of all 53 Mexican consulates in the United States, a U.S. official said, a move that could lead Secretary of State Marco Rubio to consider ordering the closure of some posts.

item.title: Former OpenAI board members, including mother of 4 Musk children, testify in trial
item.description: The high-stakes OpenAI trial pitting tech giants Elon Musk and Sam Altman against each other is wrapping up its second week with testimony from former board members. Ashley Gold, Axios senior tech policy reporter, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Trump-appointed council proposes major redesign of FEMA response role
item.description: A council appointed by President Trump has proposed major changes to FEMA's disaster relief response.

item.title: Infectious disease specialist explains why hantavirus isn't the next COVID
item.description: At least five U.S. states are monitoring passengers who were aboard the cruise ship at the center of the deadly hantavirus outbreak. Dr. Monica Gandhi, an infectious disease specialist at the University of California, San Francisco, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: AI is as a top cause of layoffs, accounting for 26% of April's job cuts
item.description: New data shows artificial intelligence is the most cited reason for layoffs, even as economists debate whether it is truly displacing workers.

item.title: Powerful tornadoes ravage hundreds of homes across Mississippi
item.description: At least 14 tornadoes touched down in Mississippi, injuring more than a dozen people and destroying hundreds of homes. CBS News correspondent Jason Allen reports from Purvis.

item.title: $60,000 CD vs. $60,000 high-yield savings account vs. $60,000 money market account: Which earns more interest now?
item.description: When comparing these three accounts and the interest-earning potential over the next year, there's one clear winner.

item.title: Is the Iranian regime really in a "state of collapse" as Trump claims?
item.description: As Trump pushes for a deal with an Iranian regime he portrays as deeply fractured, analysts say power in Tehran may be shifting, but that doesn't mean disarray.

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein's possible suicide note released by judge
item.description: A former cellmate has previously claimed that the note was written by Epstein after a failed suicide attempt less than a month before his death.

item.title: The secret to New York Liberty's staying power
item.description: The WNBA kicks off its 30th season on Friday night with a matchup between the Connecticut Sun and New York Liberty. The Liberty is one of only four original WNBA teams that have remained since 1997. Adriana Diaz went to Brooklyn to learn how the Liberty could serve as a model for all women's sports.

item.title: Should you lock a mortgage rate before the next inflation report is released?
item.description: A mortgage interest rate lock before the next inflation report is released could make sense now. Here's why.

item.title: 3 reverse mortgage advantages to know this May
item.description: A reverse mortgage could be a smart financial tool for senior homeowners this May. Here are three reasons why.

item.title: Media mogul Ted Turner | 60 Minutes Archive
item.description: In 2008, 60 Minutes correspondent Morley Safer traveled to Ted Turner's ranch in Montana, a small part of the billionaire's vast U.S. land holdings, to profile the pioneering media mogul. Turner, who died Wednesday at age 87, created CNN and launched the format of 24/7 cable news.

item.title: Analyzing video of the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting
item.description: A CBS News team analyzed the security footage of the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting. Nicole Sganga breaks down what we learned.

item.title: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
item.description: Health officials have identified at least eight confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.

item.title: Which debts can't be garnished from your Social Security? Here are 4 to know.
item.description: Social Security benefits are legally protected from creditors, but only certain ones. Here's what's off-limits.

item.title: Why is Trump claiming the U.S. won the war with Iran?
item.description: President Trump said Wednesday that he thinks the U.S. won the war with Iran. CBS News' Weijia Jiang reports.

item.title: 5 states monitoring passengers who departed ship stricken by hantavirus
item.description: American passengers who left the MV Hondius cruise ship in April are being monitored for hantavirus in at least five states, health officials said.

item.title: Dozens of cocaine packages seized in Pacific, 11 rescued from shipwreck
item.description: The Mexican navy helped rescue shipwrecked sailors and retrieve bales of illicit drugs that had been dumped into the ocean.

item.title: Gas prices top $4.50 a gallon, reaching highest level since July 2022
item.description: Gas prices are now 52% higher than prior to the Iran war, with drivers paying $1.56 more per gallon, data shows.
